Thus, in the family `M_ : ι → Subrep M`, each `M_ i` is a submodule of `M`, and if we take `S = M`, then there exists `i` with `S ≤ M_ i`, so `M ≤ M_ i`, so `M_ i = M`.

Therefore, for any `T : Submodule k M`, `T ≤ M = (M_ i).toSubmodule`.

(filterComplete : ∀ i, ∃ j, i ≤ j ∧ s j ≤ s i)

letI : IsFilterComplete (fun i ↦ (M_ i).toSubmodule) :=
    ⟨by
      -- Use the fact that the filter structure on submodules is preserved by the filter structure on subrepresentations
      have h₁ : ∀ i : ι, Filter.Tendsto (fun j : ι ↦ (M_ j).toSubmodule.castLE (le_trans (le_refl _) (M_ i).leM)) (Filter.atTop) (nhds (M_ i).toSubmodule) := by
        intro i
        -- Use the fact that the filter structure on submodules is preserved by the filter structure on subrepresentations
        have h₂ : ∀ i : ι, Filter.Tendsto (fun j : ι ↦ (M_ j).toSubmodule.castLE (le_trans (le_refl _) (M_ i).leM)) (Filter.atTop) (nhds (M_ i).toSubmodule) := by
          intro i
          convert IsFilterComplete.tendsto_atTop M_ using 1 <;>
            simp_all [Submodule.tendsto_atTop.toSubmodule]
          <;>
          aesop
        exact h₂ i
      -- Use the fact that the filter structure on submodules is preserved by the filter structure on subrepresentations
      have h₃ : Filter.Tendsto (fun i : ι ↦ (M_ i).toSubmodule.castLE (le_trans (le_refl _) (M_ i).leM)) (Filter.atTop) (nhds (⊥ : Submodule k (M : Type))) := by
        -- Use the fact that the filter structure on submodules is preserved by the filter structure on subrepresentations
        have h₄ : Filter.Tendsto (fun i : ι ↦ (M_ i).toSubmodule.castLE (le_trans (le_refl _) (M_ i).leM)) (Filter.atTop) (nhds (⊥ : Submodule k (M : Type))) := by
          convert IsFilterComplete.tendsto_atBot M_ using 1 <;>
            simp_all [Submodule.tendsto_atTop.toSubmodule]
          <;>
          aesop
        exact h₄
      -- Use the fact that the filter structure on submodules is preserved by the filter structure on subrepresentations
      have h₅ : Filter.Tendsto (fun i : ι ↦ (M_ i).toSubmodule.castLE (le_trans (le_refl _) (M_ i).leM)) (Filter.atTop) (nhds (⊥ : Submodule k (M : Type))) := by
        exact h₃
      -- Use the fact that the filter structure on submodules is preserved by the filter structure on subrepresentations
      exact
        inferInstanceAs (IsFilterComplete M_)
          (by
            -- Use the fact that the filter structure on submodules is preserved by the filter structure on subrepresentations
            have h₆ : Filter.Tendsto (fun i : ι ↦ (M_ i).toSubmodule.castLE (le_trans (le_refl _) (M_ i).leM)) (Filter.atTop) (nhds (⊥ : Submodule k (M : Type))) := by
              exact h₅
            -- Use the fact that the filter structure on submodules is preserved by the filter structure on subrepresentations
            simpa [Submodule.tendsto_atTop.toSubmodule] using h₆)
    ⟩
  inferInstance

letI := inferInstanceAs [FilterComplete (fun i ↦ (M_ i).toSubmodule)]
  exact inferInstance

have h_main : ∀ {i j : ι}, (M_ i).toSubmodule ≤ (M_ j).toSubmodule ↔ M_ i ≤ M_ j := by
    intro i j
    constructor
    · intro h
      have h₁ : (M_ i).toSubmodule ≤ (M_ j).toSubmodule := h
      exact Subrep.coe_le_coe.mp h₁
    · intro h
      have h₁ : M_ i ≤ M_ j := h
      exact Subrep.coe_le_coe.mpr h₁
  
  -- Use the fact that the submodules are ordered the same as the subreps
  -- and that IsFilterComplete is a property of the order and the indexing set.
  exact inferInstance

{
    before :
      ∀ (i j : ι), i ≤ j → (M_ i).toSubmodule ≤ (M_ j).toSubmodule :=
      by
        intro i j h
        exact Submodule.to_le.mpr (M_ i).mono h

    is_filter_complete :
      ∀ (S : Submodule k M), IsClosed S ∧ (∀ i : ι, S ≤ (M_ i).toSubmodule) → ∃ j : ι, (M_ j).toSubmodule ≤ S :=
      by
        intro S hS
        haveI : IsFilterComplete M_ := inferInstance
        have h₁ : IsClosed (S : Submodule k M) := hS.1
        have h₂ : ∀ i : ι, (M_ i : Subrep M) ≤ S.toSubrep := by
          intro i
          exact Subrep.of_to_le hS.2 i
        -- Use the fact that the subrepresentations are filter-complete to find a suitable j
        have h₃ : ∃ j : ι, (M_ j : Subrep M) ≤ S.toSubrep := (inferInstance : IsFilterComplete M_).is_filter_complete S.toSubrep ⟨by simpa using h₁, h₂⟩
        obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := h₃
        refine' ⟨j, _⟩
        simpa [Subrep.to_submodule] using hj
  }
